skill.name: scanlume-ocr-api
skill.description: Call the Scanlume public image OCR API for local screenshots, JPG files, PNG files, and image-based tables. It builds base64 data URLs, selects simple or formatted OCR, and returns text, Markdown, HTML, or structured table-aware output.
skill.summary: Call Scanlume image OCR for local images and table-aware text extraction.
skill.author: daanaagua

security_audit.summary: Static findings for hardcoded URLs, environment access, and network usage are mostly expected for an OCR API helper. The Ruby or shell execution and weak cryptography alerts are false positives from Markdown examples and OCR/API text, but the helper does read local image files, sends base64 contents to Scanlume, and uses bearer-token authentication.

medium_findings.title: Local Image Contents Sent to External OCR API
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The helper reads a local file, base64-encodes it, and posts it to the Scanlume OCR endpoint. This is the intended function, but users may upload sensitive screenshots or documents to an external service.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The code path directly shows local file bytes becoming a base64 data URL and being sent through an HTTP POST. The behavior is documented as OCR functionality, so the risk is data disclosure rather than confirmed abuse.

medium_findings.title: Bearer Token Read from CLI or Environment
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The script accepts a Scanlume API key from --api-key or SCANLUME_API_KEY and sends it as an Authorization bearer token. This is necessary for the API, but command-line secrets can leak through shell history or process listings.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The API key handling and Authorization header are explicit in the script. The risk is well understood and legitimate for an authenticated API helper.

medium_findings.title: Configurable Base URL Can Redirect Data and Token
medium_findings.description: TRUE_POSITIVE: The base URL can be overridden by --base-url or SCANLUME_BASE_URL. This supports testing, but a malicious or mistaken override would send both image contents and bearer token to a non-default endpoint.
medium_findings.confidence_reasoning: The override path is clear, but no malicious default endpoint is present. The concern depends on operator configuration or prompt-driven misuse.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded Scanlume URLs Are Expected API Documentation
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The hardcoded URLs point to the documented Scanlume website and API host used by the OCR workflow. No unrelated or covert destination was found.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Every URL reviewed is either the Scanlume public site or the Scanlume API base URL. This matches the stated purpose of the skill.

low_findings.title: Markdown Backticks Are Not Command Execution
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: Static Ruby or shell backtick alerts came from Markdown inline code and fenced examples. No shell, subprocess, eval, or Ruby execution path was found in the reviewed files.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The flagged text is documentation syntax, not executable code. The Python helper does not invoke shell commands or external processes.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography Alerts Match OCR Documentation Text
low_findings.description: FALSE_POSITIVE: The weak cryptographic algorithm alerts do not correspond to hashing, encryption, or signature code. They appear to match ordinary OCR/API documentation lines.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Reviewed locations contain OCR descriptions, API error-code names, or URLs, not cryptographic operations. No weak algorithm use was found.

dangerous_patterns.title: Network Request with User-Supplied File Data
dangerous_patterns.description: The helper posts base64-encoded local image content to an external API endpoint. This pattern requires user consent and careful handling of sensitive images.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The data flow from local file read to outbound HTTP request is direct and documented. It is dangerous only when used on sensitive files without informed consent.

dangerous_patterns.title: Credential Transmission to Configurable Endpoint
dangerous_patterns.description: The Authorization bearer token is sent to the resolved base URL, which can be changed by argument or environment variable. This is useful for testing but increases exfiltration risk if misconfigured.
dangerous_patterns.confidence_reasoning: The configurable destination and bearer header are explicit. No evidence shows malicious configuration in the repository.

content.value_statement: Image text is hard to reuse when it stays inside screenshots, scans, or table images. This skill calls Scanlume OCR and returns usable text, Markdown, HTML, or structured table-aware output.
actual_capabilities: Calls the public Scanlume image OCR endpoint for local image files.
Builds full base64 data URLs from JPG, PNG, or screenshot files.
Selects simple OCR for plain text or formatted OCR for structured output.
Returns text, Markdown, HTML, preview, or full response output from the helper script.
Documents table-aware formatted response fields, including blocks and table summaries.
Rejects PDF input in the helper because public PDF OCR is beta-gated.
limitations: Requires a Scanlume API key supplied by environment variable or command option.
Sends image contents to an external OCR service, so sensitive files need review first.
Does not support public PDF OCR, multipart upload, or remote file URL input.
OCR quality depends on image clarity, orientation, language, and table complexity.

use_cases.title: Convert Support Screenshots
use_cases.description: Extract searchable text from customer screenshots so agents can summarize issues and copy relevant fields.

use_cases.title: Capture Table Images
use_cases.description: Use formatted OCR to recover rows, columns, and table summaries from image-based reports.

use_cases.title: Prepare OCR Output for Automation
use_cases.description: Call the API helper from Claude, Codex, or Claude Code and route the selected OCR output into downstream tasks.

prompt_templates.title: Extract Plain Text
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Scanlume OCR on this local image path in simple mode and return only the extracted plain text.

prompt_templates.title: Convert a Table Image
prompt_templates.scenario: Structured extraction from an image-based table
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Scanlume OCR in formatted mode for this table screenshot. Return a Markdown table and note any uncertain cells.

prompt_templates.title: Compare Output Formats
prompt_templates.scenario: Choose the best OCR output for reuse
prompt_templates.prompt: Run formatted OCR on this image and compare the Markdown, HTML, and preview outputs. Recommend the best format for editing.

prompt_templates.title: Build an OCR Workflow
prompt_templates.prompt: Use Scanlume OCR on this batch of local image paths. For each image, choose the mode, extract the result, and flag low-confidence table structure.

output_examples.input: A screenshot of an invoice with customer details and line items.
output_examples.output: The skill returns the invoice text, preserves headings, and presents line items as a readable Markdown table.

output_examples.input: A phone photo of a printed schedule.
output_examples.output: The skill extracts event names, dates, and times, then notes rows that may need manual verification.

output_examples.input: A dashboard screenshot with several labeled metrics.
output_examples.output: The skill returns the visible labels and values as structured text suitable for a report summary.

best_practices: Use simple mode for plain text and formatted mode when layout or tables matter.
Set SCANLUME_API_KEY in the environment instead of passing secrets on the command line.
Review images for private data before sending them to the external OCR API.
anti_patterns: Do not use this skill for PDFs because the public PDF API is not generally available.
Do not send confidential screenshots unless the user approves external OCR processing.
Do not override the base URL unless you control and trust the target endpoint.

faq.question: Does this skill support PDF OCR?
faq.answer: No. The helper blocks PDFs because the public Scanlume PDF OCR route is beta-gated.

faq.question: What image formats can it process?
faq.answer: It can process local image files when Python can infer or receive the correct MIME type, such as PNG or JPG.

faq.question: When should I use formatted mode?
faq.answer: Use formatted mode for headings, multi-block screenshots, Markdown, HTML, or image-based tables.

faq.question: Where should I store the API key?
faq.answer: Use the SCANLUME_API_KEY environment variable to reduce exposure in shell history and process listings.

faq.question: Can it read images from remote URLs?
faq.answer: No. The documented public API expects a full base64 data URL, not a remote file URL.

faq.question: Is the skill safe for sensitive images?
faq.answer: Use caution. The helper sends image contents to an external OCR API, so review private data before processing.
